title: AGENTS.md
text: file defines general development rules R package. Follow rules unless task gives specific instructions.

title: AGENTS.md
text: changing code: Inspect current implementation. Read related tests. Read related documentation. Check DESCRIPTION. Follow existing package conventions. ALWAYS inspect deciding. guess package behavior, API shape, file layout, coding conventions.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Keep patches focused. Prefer small, reviewable changes. Separate unrelated work separate patches notes. refactor exposes another problem, report separately unless blocks requested task. Avoid mixing feature work, cleanup, formatting, unrelated fixes.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Write clean R code. Prefer: Small functions. Clear names. Explicit inputs. Explicit return values. Local state. Simple control flow. Use S3 behavior depends object class. Constructors validate normalize objects. Methods dispatch class. Public constructors object invariants. Name internal (non-exported) functions leading dot, e.g. .write_json(), .update_temporal_extent(). Exported functions carry dot. exception S3 generics methods, follow generic.class convention (e.g. render_style.rstatic_style_rgb) leading dot, even internal. Avoid: Hidden state. Global side effects. Implicit assumptions. Partial matching. Silent recycling parallel inputs. Broad conditional logic S3 dispatch clearer.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Use usethis devtools package work. Use roxygen2 documentation. Use testthat tests. Use namespace-qualified calls external packages: Add required dependencies : Handle optional dependencies : Optional dependencies must loaded package load time.
code: terra::rast() xml2::read_xml() usethis::use_package("pkg") requireNamespace("pkg", quietly = TRUE)

title: AGENTS.md
text: Write package documentation matter--fact style. Documentation : Clear. Factual. Concise. Readable. Specific behavior. Public functions need roxygen blocks : Use @rdname related functions S3 methods. Internal functions use: internal S3 specialized functions use “export” register specialization: Document function , arguments mean, object represents, returned, errors users expect. edit generated documentation hand. NEVER edit NAMESPACE files man/. write roxygen blocks, vignettes, README text, pkgdown articles, user-facing messages terse style used agent instructions.
code: #' @title #' @name #' @description #' @param #' @return #' @examples #' @export #' @keywords internal #' @noRd #' @export #' @keywords internal #' @noRd

title: AGENTS.md
text: New behavior needs tests. Bug fixes need regression tests practical. Test: Normal behavior. Edge cases. Object invariants. Expected errors. User-visible behavior. Use small, stable fixtures : Avoid large fixtures, local caches, generated files, temporary artifacts Git.
code: tests/testthat/ inst/extdata/

title: AGENTS.md
text: Separate: Detection. Parsing. Validation. Construction. Use public constructors enforce object invariants. Converters return package objects public constructors practical. Parsers fail clearly input unsupported.

title: AGENTS.md
text: Examples vignettes must describe actual data use. Keep examples small reproducible. Use optional dependencies conditionally. documentation, examples, vignettes, pkgdown articles change, verify build. Use: site-level documentation needs checking.
code: pkgdown::build_site()

title: AGENTS.md
text: considering work done, run: R CMD check acceptable. package must remain CRAN-clean: ERRORs, WARNINGs, avoidable NOTEs. EVERY change leave package documented, tested, checkable.
code: devtools::document() devtools::test() devtools::check()

title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: rstatic provides composable primitives build serialize static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog (STAC) documents. static catalog tree plain JSON files linked one another, following STAC specification version 1.0.0: vignette shows build tree scratch. design separates pure core builds documents memory thin /O shell: [stac_save()] writer [stac_read()] reader. Nothing written call stac_save() end. fully self-contained vignette, output written temporary directory:
code: stac/   catalog.json   collections/     {collection}/       collection.json       items/         {item}/           item.json root <- tempfile("stac-")

title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: new_catalog() builds root catalog document.
code: catalog <- new_catalog(   id = "rstatic-catalog",   title = "rstatic package catalog",   description = "An example static STAC catalog built with rstatic" ) catalog #> <STAC Catalog: rstatic-catalog> #>   title: rstatic package catalog #>   description: An example static STAC catalog built with rstatic #>   links: 2

title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: STAC, collections group related items. new_collection() builds document add_collection() registers child catalog, returning updated catalog. files written. extra named arguments (e.g. citation, doi) stored additional collection fields.
code: collection <- new_collection(   id = "land-cover",   title = "Example Land Cover",   description = "Annual land cover map (example data)",   license = "CC-BY-4.0" ) catalog <- add_collection(catalog, collection) collection #> <STAC Collection: land-cover> #>   title: Example Land Cover #>   description: Annual land cover map (example data) #>   license: CC-BY-4.0 #>   links: 3 #>   fields: description, extent, id, license, links, stac_version, title, type

title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: terra installed, extract_bbox() reads raster returns bounding box WGS84. terra available, can pass bounding box directly. small Sentinel-2 subset ships package demonstration. as_geometry() helper turns bounding box GeoJSON polygon external dependencies:
code: tif_path <- system.file(   "extdata/lulc/S2_MSI_20LMR_2022-01-05_2022-12-23_class_v1.tif",   package = "rstatic" ) bbox <- extract_bbox(tif_path) bbox #>       xmin       ymin       xmax       ymax  #> -63.636579  -8.630257 -63.418217  -8.412882 bbox <- c(-63.63658, -8.630256, -63.41822, -8.412882) geometry <- as_geometry(bbox) geometry #> <GeoJSON Geometry: Polygon> #>   bbox: -63.636580, -8.630256, -63.418220, -8.412882

title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: Items GeoJSON Features. Build properties new_properties() assets new_asset(); asset media type deduced file extension. STAC requires every item carry datetime. map annual classification covering whole 2022, better described range: pass start_datetime end_datetime leave datetime . new_properties() records datetime null, exactly specification mandates ranged items. Passing collection records owning collection’s id item, STAC requires whenever item links collection. also lets later steps locate item disk without repeating collection.
code: item <- new_item(   id = "land-cover-2022",   bbox = bbox,   geometry = geometry,   collection = collection,   properties = new_properties(     start_datetime = "2022-01-05T00:00:00Z",     end_datetime = "2022-12-23T00:00:00Z",     description = "Example land cover map for 2022"   ),   assets = list(     data = new_asset(tif_path, title = "Land Cover 2022")   ) ) item #> <STAC Item: land-cover-2022> #>   collection: land-cover #>   bbox: -63.636580, -8.630256, -63.418220, -8.412882 #>   datetime: 2022-01-05T00:00:00Z / 2022-12-23T00:00:00Z #>   assets: data #>   links: 4 #>   fields: assets, bbox, collection, geometry, id, links, properties, stac_version, type

title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: add_items() links item collection updates collection’s spatial temporal extent automatically. returns updated collection without writing anything disk. temporal extent picks item’s start_datetime/end_datetime range, interval spans full year:
code: collection <- add_items(collection, item) collection #> <STAC Collection: land-cover> #>   title: Example Land Cover #>   description: Annual land cover map (example data) #>   license: CC-BY-4.0 #>   bbox: -63.636580, -8.630256, -63.418220, -8.412882 #>   interval: 2022-01-05T00:00:00Z / 2022-12-23T00:00:00Z #>   links: 4 #>   fields: description, extent, id, license, links, stac_version, title, type

title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: new_thumbnail() describes PNG preview render raster. pure builder: reads raster writes file. returns STAC asset (like new_asset()) carrying render intent (source raster, width, style), stac_save() materializes later, owning item written. stac_style() object describes raster values map thumbnail pixels; validates normalizes rendering intent. LULC raster shipped package (S2_MSI_20LMR_2022-01-05_2022-12-23_class_v1.tif) single-band GeoTIFF classification map 2022, derived Sentinel-2 L2A data cube tile 20LMR Rondônia state, Brazil. pixel holds integer class 1 9, 0 marking nodata. data cube training points available sitsdata repository. style type inferred parameters rather chosen explicitly. Supplying values colors produces categorical style, suitable land-cover map integer class color: qml_to_style() reads supported QGIS .qml raster style returns kind object stac_style() (requires xml2). QML shipped alongside LULC raster paletted style, converts equivalent categorical style: new_thumbnail() accepts either raster path doc_asset, derive thumbnail straight item’s data asset. Attach intent "thumbnail" key, add updated item collection. raster touched yet. PNG rendered save time, requires terra, attach terra available:
code: land_cover_style <- stac_style(   values = c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9),   colors = c("#F39C12", "#CD6155", "#E0DB34", "#1E8449", "#229C59",              "#00B29E", "#3ABABA", "#2980B9", "#A0B9C8"),   labels = c("Clear_Cut_Bare_Soil", "Clear_Cut_Burned_Area",              "Clear_Cut_Vegetation", "Forest", "Mountainside_Forest",              "Riparian_Forest", "Seasonally_Flooded", "Water", "Wetland"),   nodata = 0 ) land_cover_style #> <Style: categorical> #>   labels: Clear_Cut_Bare_Soil, Clear_Cut_Burned_Area, Clear_Cut_Vegetation, Fore... (9) #>   nodata: 0 qml_style <- qml_to_style(   system.file("extdata/lulc/S2_MSI_20LMR_2022-01-05_2022-12-23_class_v1.qml",               package = "rstatic") ) qml_style #> <Style: categorical> #>   labels: Clear_Cut_Bare_Soil, Clear_Cut_Burned_Area, Clear_Cut_Vegetation, Fore... (9) thumb <- new_thumbnail(   item$assets$data,   width = 400,   style = land_cover_style ) item <- add_asset(item, "thumbnail", thumb) collection <- add_items(collection, item)

title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: far nothing written. stac_save() writer: persists exactly documents hand . pure overwrite, implicit reads merges, renders thumbnail intent item’s directory. Documents written children-first (items, collection, catalog). thumbnail attached, PNG now exists item directory. -memory asset still carries relative href "thumbnail.png", update_root() re-points rendered file root plotting: Categorical land-cover thumbnail rendered save time.
code: stac_save(catalog = catalog, collection = collection, items = item,           root_dir = root) item <- update_root(item, root) plot(item$assets$thumbnail)

title: Generating a static STAC catalog with rstatic
text: machinery renders continuous rasters. package also ships single Sentinel-2 L2A band (S2_MSI_20LMR_B04_2022-07-16.tif): red band (B04) tile 20LMR, acquired 16 July 2022. pixels surface-reflectance values scaled integers (x1000, roughly 190-1400 scene). neither values/colors three bands, stac_style() infers continuous style. grayscale ramp stretches reflectance range black white: matching QML single-band pseudocolor style, qml_to_style() converts continuous color ramp: preview band part catalog, give collection, add item, link collection catalog. Everything saved shared root, alongside land-cover collection. build item around data asset, asset feeds extract_bbox() new_thumbnail(). resolve rendered PNG update_root() plot : Pseudocolor stretch Sentinel-2 B04 (red) band.
code: b04_path <- system.file(   "extdata/s2/S2_MSI_20LMR_B04_2022-07-16.tif",   package = "rstatic" ) stac_style(min = 192, max = 1371, palette = c("black", "white")) #> <Style: continuous> #>   stretch: min=192, max=1371 #>   palette: black, white b04_style <- qml_to_style(   system.file("extdata/s2/S2_MSI_20LMR_B04_2022-07-16.qml", package = "rstatic") ) b04_style #> <Style: continuous> #>   stretch: min=192, max=1371 #>   palette: #30123b, #28bceb, #a4fc3c, #fb7e21, #7a0403 s2_collection <- new_collection(   "sentinel-2-l2a", "Sentinel-2 L2A", "Single-band reflectance preview" ) b04_asset <- new_asset(b04_path, title = "B04 (red)") b04_item <- new_item(   "b04-2022-07-16",   bbox = extract_bbox(b04_asset),   collection = s2_collection,   properties = new_properties(datetime = "2022-07-16T00:00:00Z"),   assets = list(data = b04_asset) ) b04_item <- add_asset(   b04_item, "thumbnail",   new_thumbnail(b04_item$assets$data, width = 400, style = b04_style) ) s2_collection <- add_items(s2_collection, b04_item) catalog <- add_collection(catalog, s2_collection)  stac_save(catalog = catalog, collection = s2_collection, items = b04_item,           root_dir = root)  b04_item <- update_root(b04_item, root) plot(b04_item$assets$thumbnail)

code: catalog <- stac_read(   file.path(root, "stac", "catalog.json"),   default = new_catalog("restore-plus", "Restore+ Catalog", "...") )  deforestation <- new_collection(   id = "deforestation",   title = "Example Deforestation",   description = "A second collection added in a later run" ) catalog <- add_collection(catalog, deforestation) stac_save(catalog = catalog, collection = deforestation, root_dir = root)  # The catalog now links every collection (land-cover, sentinel-2-l2a, # and deforestation). `list_links()` filters by any link field. list_links(catalog, rel == "child") #> [[1]] #> <STAC Link: child> #>   href: collections/land-cover/collection.json #>   type: application/json #>   title: Example Land Cover #>  #> [[2]] #> <STAC Link: child> #>   href: collections/sentinel-2-l2a/collection.json #>   type: application/json #>   title: Sentinel-2 L2A #>  #> [[3]] #> <STAC Link: child> #>   href: collections/deforestation/collection.json #>   type: application/json #>   title: Example Deforestation

what: Primitives to Generate Static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs
title: Primitives to Generate Static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs
text: Build browsable, server-less static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs (STAC) R. rstatic turns folder geospatial assets valid STAC catalog can publish anywhere static files served — GitHub Pages, S3 bucket, plain web server. database running service required. small, composable primitives catalogs, collections, items, assets, links. one pure builder explicit write disk, stay control gets created . makes rstatic equally useful foundation higher-level tools STAC generators experiment catalog builders. implements STAC specification version 1.0.0.

title: Primitives to Generate Static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs
text: Build catalogs, collections, items, assets, links simple constructor functions. Serialize document canonical static-catalog path disk. Track spatial temporal extents automatically items added. Optionally extract bounding boxes rasters, render PNG thumbnail assets, import QGIS layer styles. optional helpers activate automatically supporting packages installed; otherwise can still build complete catalogs supplying bounding boxes, geometries, styles .

title: Primitives to Generate Static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs
text: example builds minimal static catalog one collection one item, writing JSON tree temporary directory. resulting directory follows canonical static catalog layout, collection linking item: file self-contained STAC document. extend catalog already disk, read back stac_read(), add , save – saving pure overwrite, reading first preserves existing children.
code: library(rstatic)  root <- tempfile("stac-")  # 1. Build the documents in memory (pure -- nothing is written yet) catalog <- new_catalog(   id = "example",   title = "Example Catalog",   description = "A minimal static STAC catalog" )  collection <- new_collection(   id = "land-cover",   title = "Land Cover",   description = "Example land cover collection" )  item <- new_item(   id = "land-cover-2020",   bbox = c(-50, -10, -49, -9),   properties = new_properties(datetime = "2020-01-01T00:00:00Z"),   assets = list(     data = new_asset("land-cover-2020.tif", title = "Land Cover 2020")   ) )  # 2. Link them with the pure add_*() builders collection <- add_items(collection, item) catalog <- add_collection(catalog, collection)  # 3. Persist -- stac_save() is the only writer stac_save(catalog = catalog, collection = collection, items = item,           root_dir = root) list.files(file.path(root, "stac"), recursive = TRUE) #> [1] "catalog.json"                                           #> [2] "collections/land-cover/collection.json"                 #> [3] "collections/land-cover/items/land-cover-2020/item.json"

what: Create STAC items, properties, and assets — item_functions
title: Create STAC items, properties, and assets — item_functions
text: Pure builders building blocks STAC Item. None touch disk; use stac_save() persist. new_properties(): assembles item properties list. new_asset(): creates single STAC Asset. new_item(): creates -memory Item (GeoJSON Feature). add_items(): links one items collection, updating collection links spatio-temporal extent, returns collection. STAC requires every item carry datetime. describe time range instead single instant, supply start_datetime end_datetime omit datetime; new_properties() records datetime null, specification mandates. add_items() derives collection's temporal extent range (datetime given).

title: Create STAC items, properties, and assets — item_functions
code: new_properties(   description = NULL,   datetime = NULL,   start_datetime = NULL,   end_datetime = NULL,   start_date = NULL,   end_date = NULL,   ... )  new_asset(href, title = NULL, roles = list("data"), ...)  new_item(   id,   bbox,   geometry = NULL,   properties = new_properties(),   assets = list(),   collection = NULL,   stac_version = "1.0.0",   ... )  add_items(collection, items)

title: Create STAC items, properties, and assets — item_functions
text: description character description. datetime character RFC 3339 datetime, NULL. Recorded null omitted alongside start_datetime/end_datetime range. start_datetime character RFC 3339 start datetime, NULL. end_datetime character RFC 3339 end datetime, NULL. start_date character start date, NULL. end_date character end date, NULL. ... Additional named fields. See details function. href character asset target (path URL). title character title. roles list asset roles. Defaults list("data"). id character identifier item. bbox numeric vector c(xmin, ymin, xmax, ymax). geometry GeoJSON geometry list. NULL, derived bbox via as_geometry(). properties list item properties, e.g. new_properties(). assets named list assets, e.g. new_asset(). collection add_items(), doc_collection link items . new_item(), optional doc_collection character collection id recorded item's top-level collection field. STAC requires field whenever item carries collection link (items built always ); omitted, stac_save() stamps collection given. stac_version character STAC version. Defaults "1.0.0". items single doc_item, list doc_item objects, link collection.

title: Create STAC items, properties, and assets — item_functions

title: Describe a thumbnail asset — new_thumbnail
text: Pure builder returns STAC Asset describing PNG thumbnail rendered raster. raster read file written : render intent (source raster, width, style) carried asset materialized later, owning item written stac_save(). new_thumbnail() generic. Pass source either character path URL, doc_asset new_asset() – typically item's data asset – case raster taken asset's resolved local_path (see update_root()) href. optional style argument controls raster values mapped pixels. Build stac_style() qml_to_style(). Without style, raster rendered terra's default settings. Rendering happens save time requires optional terra package. terra available, build asset manually new_asset() instead.

what: rstatic: Primitives to Generate Static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs — rstatic-package
title: rstatic: Primitives to Generate Static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalogs — rstatic-package
text: Provides reusable primitives build serialize static SpatioTemporal Asset Catalog (STAC) documents, including catalogs, collections, items, assets, links. package implements STAC specification version 1.0.0 (https://github.com/radiantearth/stac-spec) intended foundation tools, STAC generators experiment catalog builders. Optional spatial metadata extraction thumbnail rendering supported 'terra', 'QGIS' style parsing 'xml2', packages available.

what: Save STAC documents to their canonical paths — stac_save
title: Save STAC documents to their canonical paths — stac_save
text: Writes STAC Catalog, Collection, /Item documents disk following canonical static catalog layout: Catalog    -> stac/catalog.json Collection -> stac/collections/{id}/collection.json Item       -> stac/collections/{collection}/items/{id}/item.json stac_save() writer package, pure overwrite: writes exactly documents given, disk reads implicit merging. accumulate state across runs (example catalog populated several scripts), read existing document first stac_read(), add add_*() builders, save result. Documents written children-first (items, collection, catalog) reader following parent's links always finds child already disk. Items stamped collection field collection argument already carry one; item already records collection keeps value, warning emitted value differs collection argument. asset built new_thumbnail() rendered PNG point (requires terra).

what: Define a raster style for thumbnail rendering — stac_style
title: Define a raster style for thumbnail rendering — stac_style
text: stac_style() creates normalized style object used new_thumbnail() render raster previews. style describes raster values mapped thumbnail pixels. render image. Continuous styles use min/max pmin/pmax, optionally color ramp supplied palette. RGB rendering represented passing three band names bands. Categorical styles use explicit values colors mappings. Values need sequential. Optional labels describe categorical values may used derive legends. nodata applied rendering rule rendered transparent.

title: Define a raster style for thumbnail rendering — stac_style
code: stac_style(   bands = NULL,   min = NULL,   max = NULL,   pmin = NULL,   pmax = NULL,   palette = NULL,   values = NULL,   colors = NULL,   labels = NULL,   nodata = NULL,   opacity = NULL,   gamma = NULL )

title: Define a raster style for thumbnail rendering — stac_style
text: bands Optional band name vector three band names. Three bands indicate RGB rendering. min Minimum value used continuous stretching. Must length one three. Length three allowed bands length three. max Maximum value used continuous stretching. Must length one three. Length three allowed bands length three. pmin Lower percentile used derive stretch minimum image. Must length one three. Length three allowed bands length three. pmax Upper percentile used derive stretch maximum image. Must length one three. Length three allowed bands length three. palette Color ramp continuous single-band rendering. can known palette name vector colors. values Raster values categorical rendering. colors Colors associated values. labels Optional labels associated values. Defaults .character(values). nodata Optional value render transparent. applied rendering rules. opacity Optional global opacity 0 1 valid pixels. gamma Optional gamma correction continuous rendering. must used categorical styles.

title: Define a raster style for thumbnail rendering — stac_style
text: normalized rstatic_style object. object also carries mode-specific subclass: rstatic_style_categorical, rstatic_style_continuous, rstatic_style_rgb.

title: Define a raster style for thumbnail rendering — stac_style
code: # Continuous grayscale stretch stac_style(min = 0, max = 0.5, palette = c("black", "white")) #> <Style: continuous> #>   stretch: min=0, max=0.5 #>   palette: black, white  # Single-band percentile stretch stac_style(bands = "B04", pmin = 0.02, pmax = 0.98, palette = "viridis") #> <Style: continuous> #>   bands: B04 #>   stretch: pmin=0.02, pmax=0.98 #>   palette: viridis  # RGB composite from three bands stac_style(bands = c("B04", "B03", "B02"), pmin = 0.02, pmax = 0.98) #> <Style: rgb> #>   bands: B04, B03, B02 #>   stretch: pmin=0.02, pmax=0.98  # Categorical land-cover mapping stac_style(   values = c(1, 2, 3),   colors = c("#c14d00", "#367906", "#7cc900"),   labels = c("Crop", "Forest", "Grassland"),   nodata = 0 ) #> <Style: categorical> #>   labels: Crop, Forest, Grassland #>   nodata: 0

title: Resolve a document's asset paths under a local root — update_root

title: rstatic 0.3.0.9000
text: Added update_root(), re-points document’s assets -disk locations local root directory, following canonical static catalog layout. sets asset’s local_path attribute files written stac_save() – rendered thumbnail – can resolved plot() local readers. S3 generic: doc_item paths resolve item directory (using item’s collection field), doc_collection collection directory (covering propagated collection thumbnail). new_item() gained optional collection argument (doc_collection character id) records item’s top-level collection field. STAC requires field whenever item links collection; omitted, stac_save() still stamps collection given. extract_bbox() new_thumbnail() now S3 generics. addition character path URL, accept doc_asset new_asset(), case raster read asset’s resolved local_path (see update_root()) href. Added list_links(), returns document’s links plain list filters expressions evaluated link, e.g. list_links(catalog, rel == "child"). Multiple expressions combine logical ; none, links returned. print() methods doc_collection doc_item now summarize spatial temporal extent. Collections show union bbox interval extent; items show bbox datetime prefers start_datetime/end_datetime range single datetime. now close dimmed, complete list field names.

title: rstatic 0.3.0.9000
text: new_properties() now records datetime null (rather omitting field) start_datetime/end_datetime range supplied, written item valid per STAC specification, requires datetime present. stac_save() now warns item’s collection field differs collection argument, instead silently writing item collection’s path. item’s value remains authoritative.

title: rstatic 0.3.0.9000
text: first argument new_thumbnail() renamed asset_href x support method dispatch. Calls named argument (new_thumbnail(asset_href = ...)) must updated; positional calls unaffected.

title: rstatic 0.3.0.9000
text: Reworked package vignette around shipped Sentinel-2 example data: categorical land-cover map continuous B04 (red band) preview, rendered thumbnail linked catalog collection. thumbnails now plotted resolving -disk path update_root().

title: rstatic 0.2.0.9000
text: package reorganized pure functional core thin /O shell. Constructors (new_*()) builders (add_*()) longer touch disk; reading writing happen stac_read() stac_save(). eager stac_add_*() helpers renamed add_*() (add_collection(), add_items(), add_asset(), add_link()) now pure: updates -memory document returns instead writing disk. link deduplication scoped within single rel. stac_init() removed. Use stac_read(path, default = new_catalog(...)) load existing catalog, fall back freshly built one, without writing. stac_save() now writer performs pure overwrite: persists exactly documents given, implicit disk reads merges. accumulate state across runs, read existing document first stac_read(), extend add_*() builders, save. Cross-document links (self, root, parent, relative hrefs) synthesized save time rather stored memory.

title: rstatic 0.2.0.9000
text: Added stac_read(), package’s reader. loads Catalog, Collection, Item JSON document returns corresponding -memory object, returns supplied default file exist – supporting load--create pattern without ever writing disk. new_thumbnail() now pure builder. Instead rendering immediately, returns asset carrying render intent (source raster, width, style), stac_save() materializes PNG owning item written (requires terra).

title: rstatic 0.2.0.9000
text: Replaced bundled example data Sentinel-2 L2A subset tile 20LMR (Rondônia, Brazil): categorical land-cover classification B04 (red) band, matching QGIS .qml style. Rewrote vignette README around pure-core / /O-shell workflow new example data.

title: rstatic 0.1.0.9000
text: stac_style() redesigned around explicit, backend-independent style object. type legend arguments removed. rendering mode (categorical, continuous, RGB) now inferred parameters: supply values/colors/labels categorical styles, min/max pmin/pmax (optional palette) continuous styles, three bands RGB. New bands, values, colors, labels, nodata, opacity, gamma arguments added. function now returns classed rstatic_style object instead plain list. qml_to_style() now thin converter detects QGIS raster renderer delegates stac_style(). supports paletted, singlebandgray, singlebandpseudocolor renderers fails clearly anything else, including per-class alpha, discrete pseudocolor interpolation, unsupported transparency rules.

title: rstatic 0.1.0.9000
text: new_thumbnail() applies rstatic_style object defined rendering order (nodata mask, band selection, stretch value mapping, palette/colors, opacity), support gamma correction RGB composites. Added print() method rstatic_style objects. STAC links assets now receive S3 classes (doc_link, doc_links, doc_asset), child elements classed recursively print() plot() dispatch correctly nested objects. Added print() methods doc_catalog, doc_collection, doc_item, doc_asset, doc_link, doc_links minimalist, tibble-inspired style using cli. Added plot.doc_asset() render PNG, JPEG, GeoTIFF assets. new_thumbnail() now stores generated PNG path local_path attribute thumbnails can plotted relative href.

title: rstatic 0.1.0.9000
text: package vignette now calls plot(thumb) display rendered thumbnail image. Updated _pkgdown.yml missing reference topics (plot_rstatic, stac_add_asset, stac_add_link). thumbnail style created vignette now passed new_thumbnail().

title: rstatic 0.0.0.9000
text: Primitives build serialize static STAC 1.0.0 documents: new_catalog(), new_collection(), new_item(), new_asset(), link helpers. Persistence helpers write JSON documents update parent links (stac_save(), stac_init(), stac_add_collection(), stac_add_items(), stac_add_asset(), stac_add_link()). Spatial helpers extract_bbox() as_geometry() bounding boxes GeoJSON geometries (optional terra support). Thumbnail style helpers: stac_style(), qml_to_style() (optional xml2 support), new_thumbnail() (optional terra support). Package documentation, README, vignette, pkgdown site, GitHub Actions CI workflows.
